Public Safety Power Shutoff (PSPS)

En español

Make sure your contact information on your account is up-to-date to receive outage alerts and updates. You can sign in to your account, or call 1-888-221-7070.

Some areas we serve are at an increased risk of wildfire. Turning power off in areas experiencing hazardous weather, such as extremely hot, dry and windy conditions, may be necessary to ensure the safety of your community. We take the decision to turn off power seriously, and Public Safety Power Shutoffs (PSPS) will be targeted, precise and informed by robust, real-time data about the situation on the ground.

Public Safety Power Shutoff Map

Status
  • Watch
  • Warning
  • Active
  • Restored

Input your address into the "Search Map" feature at the top left of the map to see impacts to your location.

  • Watch: These areas are being monitored for elevated weather conditions that may lead to a Public Safety Power Shutoff. These are shown in Yellow on the map.
  • Warning: These areas are being monitored for elevated weather conditions and we plan to turn off power for a Public Safety Power Shutoff. These are shown in Orange on the map.
  • Active: Power has been turned off in these areas for a Public Safety Power Shutoff. These are shown in Red on the map.
  • Restored: Power has been restored to these customers who were impacted by a Public Safety Power Shutoff. These are shown in Green on the map.
  • Active Community Resource Centers are shown on the map with Blue pin icons.

*Some customers outside of Public Safety Power Shutoff areas could be impacted by a Public Safety Power Shutoff due to the interconnected nature of the electrical system.

Community impacts

Identify local resources that can support your needs before and during a PSPS and other emergencies.

FAQ

How will Pacific Power notify me of a Public Safety Power Shutoff?

Pacific Power will notify customers as soon as possible through phone, email, text, social media and media advisories if extreme weather conditions combined with on-the-ground observation signals that a Public Safety Power Shutoff is required to safeguard customers against a wildfire from starting.

Whenever possible, we will provide advance notice of a Public Safety Power Shutoff. Although worsening conditions may require swift action, our goal is to alert public safety partners approximately 72 hours in advance, and, working in tandem with these local partners, to begin alerting customers. Customers will typically receive 48 hours’ advance notice of a Public Safety Power Shutoff.

What can I expect during a Public Safety Power Shutoff?

Timeline for a Public Safety Power Shutoff

3 to 7 days before: Hazardous weather conditions are forecasted that could lead to a Public Safety Power Shutoff. Pacific Power is actively monitoring those conditions.

Up to 2 days before: Once extreme weather conditions and other hazardous variables (such as extreme drought) are met and sustained, Pacific Power begins coordinating with local officials. Customers receive first notification of a possible Public Safety Power Shutoff.

1 day before: Continue monitoring extreme wildfire conditions. Coordination between Pacific Power and local officials continues. Customers in potential shutoff area receive additional notice including why a Public Safety Power Shutoff might occur, number of customers potentially impacted, area impacted, potential event timeline and if Community Resource Centers are opening.

Power shutoff: Extreme wildfire conditions remain and on-the-ground coordination continues. Customers in affected area receive third notification that power will be turned off to prevent wildfire ignition.

Power restoration: Extreme wildfire conditions have lessened. Pacific Power crews patrol lines during daylight hours to inspect for damage and hanging debris. Power is restored once all lines are cleared and once repairs are made. Customers receive a fourth notification that power has been restored.

What could trigger a Public Safety Power Shutoff?

We look at a range of factors before considering using a Public Safety Power Shutoff for community safety.

  • Dry trees and brush and other potential wildfire fuel
  • High, sustained winds
  • Extremely low humidity
  • Current and forecasted weather conditions from multiple third parties and our own weather stations 
  • Population density
  • Real-time observation from on-the-ground experts
  • Input from local public safety and health agencies

No single factor, such as high winds, would necessitate a Public Safety Power Shutoff.

How often will Public Safety Power Shutoffs happen?

While it is impossible to predict the long-term impacts of climate change on future wildfire seasons, we do not expect Public Safety Power Shutoffs to be a common occurrence. To date, there have been only three PSPS events throughout Pacific Power’s service area — in Weed, California in 2020; Dunsmuir, California in 2021; and in parts of Oregon in September 2022.

What areas could experience a potential Public Safety Power Shutoff?

The growth in our forecasting capabilities has allowed us to mature beyond the use of pre-drawn analog maps for determining when to deploy a local Public Safety Power Shutoff (PSPS).

Now, as high-risk weather events increasingly happen in areas where they haven't typically occurred, we have the forecasting power and flexibility to issue a PSPS wherever it is necessary to keep customers and communities safe.

Our new flexibility to use a PSPS throughout our service area does not increase the likelihood that we will use one in any particular location. It just means that we have the tools to see potential wildfire conditions coming ahead of time — wherever those conditions occur, regardless of whether the area is historically high-risk or low-risk for wildfires.

How long could a Public Safety Power Shutoff last?

A number of variables are used to predict how long a Public Safety Power Shutoff would last, based on real-time factors. The shutoff would continue until the risk has passed, after which line inspection, repairs and power restoration will occur during daylight hours.

How will Pacific Power communicate with me before, during and after a Public Safety Power Shutoff event?

We will use all communication channels available to us to notify customers. This includes outbound calls, text messages, social media updates, media advisories, website banners and alerts.

Sign up for alerts and update your contact information so we can reach you. 

What if I’m dependent on electricity for a medical device?

If you rely on electricity- or battery-dependent medical technologies such as breathing machines, a power wheelchair or scooter, and home oxygen or dialysis, it is critical that you have a plan in place for a power outage during all seasons, year-round. 

Pacific Power has programs for customers with  electricity-dependent medical needs. Visit our accessibility programs page for information about these programs. You can also contact us at 1-888-221-7070 or use our contact form to let us know about your medical situation. 

You should also determine if a portable generator or portable power station is right for your home and learn how to safely use these sources of backup power generation. Customers may be eligible for rebates on qualifying equipment. 

Pacific Power also encourages you to identify local resources that can support your needs during outages and other emergencies.

Why did you change the previous system of using pre-designated PSPS areas and maps?

The short answer is we don’t need them anymore, because our forecasting capabilities have matured to the point that we can see threats coming in real-time, throughout our service area. Likewise, we’ve expanded our ability to respond to threats wherever they occur, by activating a Public Safety Power Shutoff precisely when and where it is necessary to reduce risk and to keep customers and communities safe.

Wildfire Safety For Kids with Patch and Willa

Critical facilities & critical infrastructure

Pacific Power provides prioritized restoration, backup power evaluation, additional communications and other resources before and during Public Safety Power Shutoff events to critical facility customers who provide services that are essential to public safety. 

See more on critical facilities & critical infrastructure

请致电 888-221-7070，向客服专员了解森林火灾安全与防范详情。

請致電 888-221-7070，向客服專員瞭解森林火災安全與防範詳情。

Unsere Mitarbeiter im Kundenservice beraten Sie gerne zu Waldbrandvorsorge und -bekämpfung. Sie erreichen uns unter 888-221-7070.

Lub chaw sawv cev saib xyuas tub lag luam yuav sib tham nrog koj txog ntawm kev nyab xeeb txog hluav taws hav zoov kub thiab kev npaj txhij. Thov hu rau 888-221-7070.

Ang isang ahente ng pangangalaga sa customer ay maaaring makipag-usap sa iyo tungkol sa kaligtasan at paghahanda sa mabilis na kumakalat na sunog o wildfire. Mangyaring tumawag sa 888-221-7070.

Một đại diện chăm sóc khách hàng có thể trao đổi với quý vị về an toàn cháy rừng và cách chuẩn bị sẵn sàng cho cháy rừng. Vui lòng gọi 888-221-7070.
